title: anthyllis vulneraria proles alpestris
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria proles alpestris, also known as Alpine Kidney Vetch, is a subspecies of the kidney vetch plant, native to high-altitude mountainous regions in Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as reduce swelling and promote wound healing. The medicinal properties of Alpine Kidney Vetch include its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce pain and inflammation, and improve digestion, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Alpine Kidney Vetch can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or topical cream, and is often used to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds, as well as to support overall health and well-being.
